<h1 class="article-heading text-headline-400">'It's Always Been Women': The Flagstaff Restaurants That Keep Route 66 Going</h1>
<p class="article-subheading text-utility-300">At MartAnne's and Miz Zip's, two Flagstaff institutions on Route 66, the recipes change hands but the women never stop cooking.</p>
<div id="mntl-article-meta-dynamic_1-0" class="comp mntl-article-meta-dynamic mntl-article-meta mntl-block" data-tracking-container="true"><div id="bylines_1-0" class="comp bylines allrecipes-bylines mntl-bylines text-utility-200"><div id="mntl-bylines__group_1-0" class="comp mntl-bylines__group mntl-block mntl-bylines__group--author"><div id="mntl-bylines__item_1-0" class="comp mntl-bylines__item mntl-attribution__item mntl-attribution__item--has-date">

<figcaption id="primary-image__figcap_1-0" class="comp primary-image__figcap mntl-figure-caption figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-owner">Credit: <p>Getty Images / Discover Flagstaff / Kailey Whitman</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block structured-content" data-sc-sticky-offset="90" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="4" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1250, 1550, 1950, 2350, 2750, 3150, 3550, 3950]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><p id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The pungent smell of roasting garlic, onion, and red chili simmering in big pots of beans and <a class="recommendation-inline-link" href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/16431/red-enchilada-sauce/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">red enchilada sauce</a> wafts in the air as Tina Martinez walks through the alley to get to MartAnne’s in Flagstaff, Arizona. Tomorrow, she’s at Miz Zip’s just 10 minutes down the road, where the smell of char from hamburgers, hash browns, and <a class="recommendation-inline-link" href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/150306/the-best-chicken-fried-steak/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2" rel="noopener noreferrer">chicken-fried steak</a> lingers outside.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Martinez owns both of these classic Flagstaff restaurants. Her connection to local culture dates back decades to when her mom, Anne Sedillo, was a waitress who worked the breakfast shift because it was an easier routine to manage while she was raising her kids.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I grew up seeing some of the best breakfasts all the time because I would always be at my mom's jobs early in the morning,” said Martinez. “I can remember when I was four or five years old, sitting in a booth, waiting for my grandma to pick me up and take me to school.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I [love] to be a part of [the Route 66 culture] through something that I enjoy: creating beautiful, delicious breakfast dishes,” she adds. Not only did she carry on a tradition, but she also added her own personal touches, which keep these restaurants both tied to the past and moving toward the future.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">
<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:75.0%;">

<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_1-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p>Discover Flagstaff</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<span class="heading-toc" id="toc-carrying-on-a-legacy"></span><h2 id="mntl-sc-block_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-heading mntl-sc-block-heading"> <span class="mntl-sc-block-heading__text text-title-300"> Carrying on a Legacy </span> </h2>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> MartAnne’s was originally named Martan’s Burrito Palace and owned by Gloria Martan, a Hispanic woman who grew up in Arizona. It was one of the first restaurants in the area to sell <a class="recommendation-inline-link" href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/230156/traditional-chilaquiles/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">chilaquiles</a> in the 1980s. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_12-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “Nobody could pronounce [chilaquiles],” said Martinez. “People were comfortable with <a class="recommendation-inline-link" href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/8418880/huevos-rancheros-bowl/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">rancheros</a> because rancheros were more of an Americanized idea, but nobody could say 'chilaquiles.’ The description was just so weird, like corn tortillas, scrambled with eggs, sauce, cheese, but [Gloria Martan] did it. She put it on the menu, and it became like a cult following in Flagstaff.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_15-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">
<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:75.0%;">
<img
data-src="https://www.allrecipes.com/thmb/j6ho8m6923206cYyotPaRqAHQa0=/1500x0/filters:no_upscale():max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/ar-r66-MartAnnes-chilequiles-7-4x3-ff5fe1e74b4f4a65809f2a947bb166fb.jpg"
width="4000"
height="3001"

<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_2-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p>Discover Flagstaff</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_16-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In 2001, Martinez's mother bought the restaurant with her dad’s help and changed the name to MartAnne’s, a combination of Gloria Martan and Anne Sedillo’s names. Eventually, she added her own flair to the menu by jazzing up the original recipes with more garlic and spice. For example, they used to sell only red enchilada sauce, but she added green sauce. Then she added sour cream and cilantro to the <a href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/8691/chicken-enchiladas-i/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">enchiladas</a>, which is the base of one of their popular breakfast dishes. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_17-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_18-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “We’d have diced green chilies, diced green onions, and diced tomatoes in our prep line table. We’d have all these colors, and [my mom would] figure out how to get every color onto every dish, not in the same way.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_19-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_20-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> That led to a lot of success, but Sedillo couldn’t keep up with the demand, and she wanted to sell. Martinez was about to apply to nursing school when a local doctor, who worked at the same hospital as she did, approached her and said, “It would be horrible if that restaurant leaves your family." 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_21-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_22-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> About a year later, to accommodate the increased interest and growth, Martinez moved MartAnne’s to a location around the block on Route 66, which had more visibility and five times the seating. She got to work, carrying on the traditions her mom taught her.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_23-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_24-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Then, in 2019, Martinez heard that Miz Zip’s, an American restaurant where she got her first job as a waitress at the age of 14, was for sale. She missed American food, so she talked to the owners, and soon enough, she and her husband bought Miz Zip’s. Early on, they kept the menu the same, including the original Zip burger, to retain customers. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_25-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_26-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">

</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_27-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “You’re talking the '50s, so there weren't too many things [on the menu]. If you had chicken-fried steak, you were doing a little more than the places that only had bacon and eggs,” said Martinez. Eventually, they added more flavors, such as green chile, and made the menu more diverse by adding enchiladas and tacos. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_28-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<span class="heading-toc" id="toc-keeping-route-66-history-alive-through-food"></span><h2 id="mntl-sc-block_29-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-heading mntl-sc-block-heading"> <span class="mntl-sc-block-heading__text text-title-300"> Keeping Route 66 History Alive Through Food </span> </h2>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_30-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Aside from the local culture, a big part of the Route 66 history has to do with the women of the area. “The story for us at MartAnne’s is that it's always been women,” said Martinez. “Any woman who worked in that kitchen added their flavor, their touch.” 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_31-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_32-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Gloria Martan was the original owner, and she brought her own Hispanic flair. When Sedillo took over the restaurant, one condition was that Miss Alice, a Black cook and daughter of sharecroppers, remained on staff. It was something Sedillo was more than happy to agree to, because Miss Alice was a local icon who contributed recipes to the menu, such as the <a href="https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/245359/pork-chili-verde-green-pork-chili/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">green chile pork</a> recipe. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_33-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_34-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-portrait figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">
<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:133.3%;">

</figcaption></figure>
<span class="heading-toc" id="toc-continuing-the-tradition"></span><h2 id="mntl-sc-block_35-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-heading mntl-sc-block-heading"> <span class="mntl-sc-block-heading__text text-title-300"> Continuing the Tradition  </span> </h2>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_36-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In addition to juggling the three restaurants, Martinez has six children. She knows her schedule isn’t sustainable, but she has a team of employees that keep the restaurants running well. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_37-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_38-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> For now, Martinez is keeping the traditions strong. For example, she recently remade the original sign at Miz Zip’s, a project that took two years to get approved. “I'll get text messages from people, ‘Oh my God, the new zip sign, I remember that!’” says Martinez.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_39-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_40-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block allrecipes-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">

</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_41-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In the near future, they’ll do a small remodel at Miz Zip’s, but plan to keep the paneling on the wall and as much of the interior the same as possible the same. The restaurant recently received a review saying, "It's like eating inside your grandma's kitchen, your grandma's house." 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_42-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_43-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I screenshot it, sent it to my mom, and said, 'Yep, this is exactly the vibe we knew we wanted to keep,’” said Martinez. “When we do the kitchen remodel next year, we have to keep everything as close as possible to how it is [now].”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_44-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_46-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Martinez hopes that one or more of her kids will follow in her footsteps,  just like she did. “I would love for [them to learn] the recipes and history behind the dishes,” says Martinez. “Restaurants aren’t for the faint-hearted. If one of them enjoys it, they will have all my support in keeping the legacy going.”
</p>

for (const mutation of mutationList) {
if (mutation.type === 'childList') {

return;
